raw:
{
  "judge_id": "LIRA_abstract_judge",
  "active_set_recognized": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "scores": {
    "R1.1": 5,
    "R1.2": 5,
    "RX.1": 5,
    "RX.3": 5,
    "RX.4": 5,
    "RX.5": 5
  },
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The agent names two load-bearing conceptual issues: the contradiction in the 'statelessness' claim and the confounded attribution of the performance gains. It proposes a robust reframing that separates the system into two distinct components (router and advisor), clarifies the state-handling mechanism to resolve the contradiction, and proposes an ablation study to cleanly attribute gains. This is a significant conceptual improvement.",
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the short argument chain of the abstract end-to-end and flags the two most critical, load-bearing inconsistencies: (1) claiming statelessness while describing the use of stateful histograms, and (2) attributing the headline result to the router in one paragraph and to the advisor in another. It proposes minimal, correct repairs for both issues.",
    "RX.1": "The agent provides an exemplary 'Uncertainty disclosure' section. For each piece of information it had to invent to make the text concrete (the ablation split, the specific baseline rule, the accuracy metric), it explicitly flags the invention as a plausible placeholder and instructs the user to substitute real data, marking the missing evidence perfectly.",
    "RX.3": "The agent's output is perfectly aligned with the user's request to 'Make this stronger.' It correctly interprets this as a request for substantive critique and revision of a technical abstract and delivers exactly that, without any scope drift.",
    "RX.4": "The output is not only harm-free but actively reduces downstream harm. It identifies and fixes logical contradictions and confounded experimental claims in the original draft. These are precisely the kinds of flaws that would cause a research paper to be rejected, so fixing them is a significant harm-avoidance measure.",
    "RX.5": "The output's structure provides perfect traceability. The numbered critique points serve as an explicit 'changelog,' giving a clear reason for every major change made in the revised version. The uncertainty disclosure section further annotates the source of specific placeholder values, distinguishing them from information present in the original artifact."
  },
  "confidence": {
    "R1.1": 0.9,
    "R1.2": 0.9,
    "RX.1": 0.9,
    "RX.3": 0.9,
    "RX.4": 0.9,
    "RX.5": 0.9
  },
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.1: The agent names two load-bearing conceptual issues: the contradiction in the 'statelessness' claim and the confounded attribution of the performance gains. It proposes a robust reframing that separates the system into two distinct components (router and advisor), clarifies the state-handling mechanism to resolve the contradiction, and proposes an ablation study to cleanly attribute gains. This is a significant conceptual improvement.
rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the short argument chain of the abstract end-to-end and flags the two most critical, load-bearing inconsistencies: (1) claiming statelessness while describing the use of stateful histograms, and (2) attributing the headline result to the router in one paragraph and to the advisor in another. It proposes minimal, correct repairs for both issues.
rationale.RX.1: The agent provides an exemplary 'Uncertainty disclosure' section. For each piece of information it had to invent to make the text concrete (the ablation split, the specific baseline rule, the accuracy metric), it explicitly flags the invention as a plausible placeholder and instructs the user to substitute real data, marking the missing evidence perfectly.
rationale.RX.3: The agent's output is perfectly aligned with the user's request to 'Make this stronger.' It correctly interprets this as a request for substantive critique and revision of a technical abstract and delivers exactly that, without any scope drift.
rationale.RX.4: The output is not only harm-free but actively reduces downstream harm. It identifies and fixes logical contradictions and confounded experimental claims in the original draft. These are precisely the kinds of flaws that would cause a research paper to be rejected, so fixing them is a significant harm-avoidance measure.
rationale.RX.5: The output's structure provides perfect traceability. The numbered critique points serve as an explicit 'changelog,' giving a clear reason for every major change made in the revised version. The uncertainty disclosure section further annotates the source of specific placeholder values, distinguishing them from information present in the original artifact.
